channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: In California's 'earthquake country,' a 7.0 temblor prompts confusion and a tsunami warning
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Valerie Starkey was driving through Northern California to visit relatives when she suddenly felt shaking and feared her car had broken down, only to realize later that it was an earthquake so powerful that it triggered a tsunami warning for hundreds of miles of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: South Korea's governing party head supports suspending Yoon's powers, making impeachment more likely
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s governing party chief expressed support Friday for suspending the constitutional powers of President Yoon Suk Yeol for imposing martial law this week, in a bombshell reversal that makes Yoon’s impeachment more likely.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge upholds use of race in Naval Academy admissions, saying a diverse military is stronger
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge on Friday ruled that the U.S. Naval Academy can continue considering race in its admissions process, finding that military cohesion and other national security factors mean the school should not be subjected to the same standards as civilian universities.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about Northern California's rare tsunami warning
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Northern California was under a rare and brief tsunami warning alert Thursday that tested local emergency notification systems after a 7.0 magnitude earthquake shook part of the state.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indianapolis police officers are acquitted in the 2022 death of a man at his parents’ home
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Two Indianapolis police officers were acquitted of manslaughter and other charges Friday in the death of a man after officers shocked him with a Taser and restrained him face down while handcuffing him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Johnson woos his sharpest critics in preparation for Speaker vote
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is wooing his sharpest critics in the House GOP ahead of his official election for Speaker on the House floor, working to eliminate opposition and secure the strongest leadership mandate possible in a razor-thin majority. In the latest development, Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Crews recover the body of a woman from a Pennsylvania sinkhole after a 4-day search
item.description: Police say the remains of a woman who fell into a sinkhole have been recovered four days after she went missing while searching for her cat.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Photos show face of 'person of interest' wanted in shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: The NYPD released the photos amid a city-wide manhunt for the gunman who shot UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson on Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, KTLA 5

item.title: Wind-driven brushfire at Baltimore wood recycling yard closes expressway, light rail, schools
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Firefighters were battling a massive, wind-driven brushfire at a Baltimore wood recycling yard that closed a section of the expressway into the city’s downtown, the neighboring light rail line and nearby roads and schools on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Daniel Penny manslaughter charge dismissed as jury breaks for weekend
item.description: Daniel Penny jurors struggled to reach consensus on top charge, manslaughter, which carries a max sentence of 15 years in prison for the death of Jordan Neely.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tyre Sampson's family awarded $310 million after fatal fall on Florida ride
item.description: Tyre Sampson, 14, was tragically killed after falling to his death off a freefall tower drop ride at Orlando’s ICON Park.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, ABC 4

item.title: Walz says he was 'a little surprised' by election loss
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), Vice President Kamala Harris' running mate, reflected on the Democratic Party's loss in the presidential election, saying he was "a little surprised" by the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KTLA 5

item.title: Suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO shooting used fake ID at hostel: sources
item.description: The man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in Midtown is believed to have used a fake New Jersey ID to check into an Upper West Side hostel before the shooting, sources told Nexstar's WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Company fined $172K after children employed to clean Iowa pork plant
item.description: They employed 11 children to "use corrosive cleaners to clean head splitters, jaw pullers, bandsaws, neck clippers and other equipment.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO uncorks anger at insurance industry
item.description: The killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson on a Manhattan street is the latest moment to shine a light on the tensions roiling American life.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing spotlights challenge of protecting leaders of companies
item.description: He’s one of the most famous and widely admired corporate leaders in the world. But it’s the haters that companies like Mark Zuckerberg’s Meta worry about.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Splash pads associated with waterborne disease outbreaks: CDC
item.description: "Children are at increased risk for exposure to pathogens in contaminated splash pad water," the CDC said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New clues emerge as investigators hunt for the gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New clues emerged Thursday in the hunt for the masked gunman who stalked and killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, including possible leads about his travel before the shooting and a message scrawled on ammunition found at the crime scene.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: JonBenét Ramsey Netflix doc director urges police to DNA test
item.description: No one has ever been charged in the case, though multiple suspects have been investigated over the years.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, KRON4

item.title: Father of victim in NYC subway chokehold case sues defendant Daniel Penny
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The father of the victim at the center of the fatal New York City subway chokehold trial has sued the defendant as a Manhattan jury continued to deliberate the case Thursday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NASA's stuck astronauts hit 6 months in space. Just 2 more to go
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — Known across the globe as the stuck astronauts, Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams hit the six-month mark in space Thursday with two more to go.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US judge rejects Boeing's plea deal in a conspiracy case stemming from fatal plane crashes
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday rejected a deal that would have let Boeing plead guilty to a felony conspiracy charge and pay a fine for misleading U.S. regulators about the 737 Max jetliner before two of the planes crashed, killing 346 people. U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KOENIG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: QVC recalls 1.1 million oven gloves after more than 90 burn injury reports
item.description: QVC is recalling more than 1 million pairs of oven gloves after dozens of people were burned while using them, the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission has announced.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: Link to adult website on Mattel's 'Wicked' dolls at center of proposed class action lawsuit
item.description: A mother claims she and her daughter visited the link on the box, only to be "horrified" by what they saw, according to the lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Things to know about the federal investigation into the Memphis Police Department
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A U.S. Department of Justice investigation found that the Memphis Police Department committed a host of civil rights violations, including using excessive force, making illegal traffic stops and disproportionately targeting Black people in the majority Black city.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Secret Service leader, GOP rep get into shouting match over 9/11 questioning
item.description: Acting Secret Service Director Ronald Rowe and Rep. Pat Fallon (R-Texas) engaged in a shouting match that persisted over gavel banging, as Rowe accused the lawmaker of politicizing the Sept. 11 attack.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield reverses decision to cap anesthesia coverage after a set time limit
item.description: Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield said in a statement that its decision to backpedal resulted from “significant widespread misinformation” about the policy.
item.dc:creator: Melanie Higgins, NewsNation

item.title: Underground voids from abandoned mines pose danger to people and property when land collapses
item.description: Swaths of Pennsylvania and many other states are honeycombed with old, unstable mines that can cause the earth to suddenly give way and threaten people and property.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL RUBINKAM and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police hunt for UnitedHealthcare CEO's masked killer after 'brazen, targeted' attack on NYC street
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A gunman killed UnitedHealthcare’s CEO on Wednesday in a “brazen, targeted attack” outside a Manhattan hotel where the health insurer was holding its investor conference, police said, setting off a massive search for the fleeing assailant hours before the annual Rockefeller Center Ch
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, KAREN MATTHEWS and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump names Billy Long as IRS head
item.description: Trump said his relationship with Long, who currently works as a business and tax adviser, dates back to 2011.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Stadium-size asteroid to make closest approach to Earth
item.description: The stadium-size asteroid is considered a "potentially hazardous object," but poses no threat to Earth this year.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: British indie rock band robbed at gunpoint in California '10 minutes into US tour,' group says
item.description: Sports Team, a six-piece London-based alternative rock band, had stopped for coffee when someone alerted them that their van was being ransacked by robbers, according to their latest Instagram post.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, Fox 5

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO fatally shot in Midtown: NYPD
item.description: MIDTOWN, Manhattan (PIX11) – The CEO of UnitedHealthcare was killed in a targeted shooting in Midtown Wednesday morning, police said. Brian Thompson, 50, was shot in the back and leg by a masked gunman just before 7 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rescuers search for woman who may have fallen into a sinkhole while looking for her lost cat
item.description: UNITY TOWNSHIP, Pa. (AP) — Rescuers are searching for a woman who went looking for her lost cat and apparently fell into a sinkhole that opened above an abandoned western Pennsylvania coal mine.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and GENE PUSKAR Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Frontier Airlines to roll out first-class seating, other perks in 2025
item.description: Frontier Airlines will offer first-class seating for the first time in its history starting in late 2025, the company announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, ABC 4

item.title: US biathletes fear retaliation for speaking out about sexual harassment, report finds
item.description: U.S. biathletes face “misogynistic” behavior while racing and fear retaliation if they report their concerns, according to an inquiry by an independent contractor hired to assess athlete safety. The investigation of U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump warns of 'hell to pay' if Hamas doesn't release hostages
item.description: President-elect Trump on Monday threatened to unleash “hell” in the Middle East if hostages held by Hamas in the Gaza Strip are not released before his inauguration Jan.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9
